question: Explain the schematic diagram of the secondary side equipment related to a single NPM (Nuclear Power Module).

nuscale_ch01_0510.title: DSRS 11.5, Rev 0: Process and Effluent Radiological Monitoring Instrumentation and Sampling SystemsII.7 Descriptions of design features and instrumentation used in primary and secondary coolant system leakage detection
nuscale_ch01_0510.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for radiological monitoring and sampling systems, specifically addressing the design features and instrumentation used for detecting leakage in primary and secondary coolant systems. It notes that most criteria are met, with some site-specific operational program requirements deferred to the COL applicant, and partial conformance for certain post-TMI action plan items related to testing and operational programs.

nuscale_ch01_0069.title: Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams
nuscale_ch01_0069.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation and control (I&C), and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information. It lists the relevant diagrams and legends provided for both I&C and piping and instrumentation diagrams, and notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must supply additional site-specific diagrams and legends as needed.

nuscale_ch01_0058.title: Steam Generator Thermal-Hydraulic Performance Testing - Electrically Heated Facility
nuscale_ch01_0058.summary: This section describes the critical heat flux (CHF) testing conducted for the NuFuel HTP2™ fuel design, which uses AREVA HMP™/HTP™ spacer grid technology. The tests, performed at the AREVA Karlstein Thermal Hydraulics facility, aimed to gather CHF data, validate and develop CHF correlations for NuScale’s reactor application, and collect additional thermal-hydraulic performance data under various operating conditions using an electrically heated 5x5 fuel bundle simulator.

nuscale_ch01_0018.title: Principal Design Criteria
nuscale_ch01_0018.summary: The Principal Design Criteria section describes the NuScale plant’s cooling and power generation systems. It outlines the flow of heat from the reactor core through the coolant and steam generators, the production of superheated steam to drive turbines and generate electricity, and the role of the Site Cooling Water System (SCWS) in supporting auxiliary systems and heat removal. Equipment design and cooling tower details are site-specific and further explained in referenced sections.

nuscale_ch01_0116.title: Figure 1.7-3e: Piping and Instrumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0116.summary: This section provides a comprehensive list of legends and abbreviations used in NuScale’s Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs), detailing the symbols for various analysis functions, measurement types, and instrumentation identifiers. It serves as a reference to interpret the diagrams by defining acronyms for sensors, analyzers, and measurement methods related to process variables such as flow, radiation, chemical composition, and physical properties.

nuscale_ch01_0774.title: SRP 15.6.3, Rev 2: Radiological Consequences of Steam Generator Tube Failure (PWR)II (No Number) Last two sentences of the last paragraph of Section II on page 15.6.32 - Plant-Specific Technical Specifications for Primary and Secondary Coolant System Iodine Activity
nuscale_ch01_0774.summary: The NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report partially conforms to the guidance regarding technical specifications for primary and secondary coolant iodine activity, as the radiological acceptance criteria are superseded by SRP Section 15.0.3. Therefore, while the technical specification requirements apply, the specific radiological criteria from the referenced section do not, as they are replaced by those in SRP Section 15.0.3.

nuscale_ch01_0068.title: Electrical and Instrumentation and Control Drawings
nuscale_ch01_0068.summary: This section explains that simplified electrical, instrumentation, and control (I&C) drawings are included in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information alongside the written text. It references tables and figures listing and explaining the symbols used in these diagrams, and notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must provide additional site-specific diagrams and legends as needed.

nuscale_ch01_0451.title: BTP 10-2, Rev 4: Design Guidelines for Avoiding Water Hammers in Steam GeneratorsPSGD B.1 thru B.4Preheat Steam Generator Designs
nuscale_ch01_0451.summary: This section explains that the NuScale plant design does not use top-feed, preheat, or once-through steam generator designs, so the regulatory guidelines for avoiding water hammers in these systems are not applicable. Instead, NuScale uses a passive natural circulation system (DHRS) that fulfills a similar safety function without the need for auxiliary feedwater pumps or the design features addressed by these guidelines.

nuscale_ch01_0452.title: BTP 10-2, Rev 4: Design Guidelines for Avoiding Water Hammers in Steam GeneratorsOTSGD B.1 Once-Through Steam Generator Designs - Auxiliary Feedwater Supply
nuscale_ch01_0452.summary: This section explains that the NuScale plant design does not use the steam generator configurations (top-feed, preheat, or once-through with auxiliary feedwater) addressed by BTP 10-2, Rev 4, and therefore the associated water hammer guidelines are not applicable. Instead, NuScale uses a passive Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) that fulfills a similar safety function without the need for auxiliary feedwater pumps or external supply headers, inherently avoiding the water hammer issues targeted by these guidelines.

nuscale_ch01_0112.title: Figure 1.7-3a: Piping and Instrumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0112.summary: This section provides the legend for piping and instrumentation diagrams (P&IDs) used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, detailing the symbols and notations for various valves, vents, drains, fire safety equipment, and specialty items. It specifies standard representations, such as 0.75" normally closed valves for all vents and drains unless otherwise noted, and includes notes on symbol usage and system abbreviations.

nuscale_ch01_0117.title: Figure 1.7-3f: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0117.summary: This section provides the legend for symbols used in NuScale’s Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs), detailing the graphical representations for various instruments, sensors, and auxiliary devices such as flow meters, pressure and temperature sensors, and sample probes. It serves as a reference to interpret the types and configurations of instrumentation shown in the diagrams.

nuscale_ch01_0111.title: Figure 1.7-2: Instrumentatio n and Controls Symbol Legend
nuscale_ch01_0111.summary: This section provides a legend explaining the symbols used for instrumentation and controls in NuScale’s safety analysis diagrams. It defines the logic gate functions, control room indicators, switch types, and other symbols relevant to system monitoring and operation, ensuring clear interpretation of the diagrams. Additionally, it includes notes on piping and instrumentation diagram conventions, such as vent and drain representations.

edges.description: Section A specifies the regulatory requirements and descriptions for instrumentation used in secondary coolant system leakage detection, while Section B provides the detailed piping and instrumentation diagrams that visually represent the implementation of these systems for a single NPM.

Section A describes the instrumentation and design features for detecting leakage in the secondary coolant system, while Section B provides testing data and performance validation for the steam generator, which is a key component of the secondary side equipment referenced in Section A.

Section A describes the instrumentation and design features for detecting leakage in the secondary coolant system, while Section B provides a general description of the secondary side equipment (such as the Site Cooling Water System) that would be monitored by such instrumentation, thus Section A specifies the monitoring requirements for the equipment described in Section B.

Section A describes the design features and instrumentation used for leakage detection in the secondary coolant system, while Section B provides the diagram legends necessary to interpret the instrumentation symbols referenced in such schematic diagrams.

Section A describes the design features and instrumentation for detecting leakage in the secondary coolant system, which directly supports the technical specifications for secondary coolant iodine activity discussed in Section B.
Section A provides schematic diagrams and drawings of secondary side equipment, which visually represent the system whose thermal-hydraulic performance is tested and analyzed in Section B.

Section A provides detailed schematic diagrams and drawings that visually clarify the design and operation of secondary side equipment, which supports and specifies the general functional descriptions and criteria outlined in Section B regarding the plant's cooling water systems and process overview.

Section A references the use of figures such as piping and instrumentation diagrams for visual clarification, while Section B provides the legend necessary to interpret the symbols and identifiers used in those diagrams.

Section A provides detailed schematic diagrams and drawings of secondary side equipment, which serve as the basis for evaluating compliance with the technical specifications for secondary coolant system iodine activity described in Section B.

Section A provides detailed testing data and validation for the fuel design's thermal-hydraulic performance, which supports the general plant and system descriptions in Section B by ensuring that the secondary side equipment (such as the steam generator) operates within safe and validated parameters.

Section A discusses testing and data collection for fuel design performance, while Section B provides diagram legends necessary to interpret schematic diagrams of secondary side equipment, thus Section B's information is required to understand or explain diagrams referenced or implied in Section A.

Section A provides test data and correlations for the thermal-hydraulic performance of the steam generator, which supports Section B's regulatory evaluation of radiological consequences and technical specifications for secondary coolant system iodine activity in the event of a steam generator tube failure.

Section A provides a general description of the secondary side equipment and systems (such as the Site Cooling Water System) relevant to a single NPM, while Section B provides the diagram legends necessary to interpret the schematic diagrams of such equipment.

Section A describes the design and function of the secondary side equipment (such as the Site Cooling Water System) relevant to the NPM, while Section B references regulatory requirements for secondary coolant system iodine activity that the described equipment must help satisfy.

Section A provides the legend for instrumentation and function identifiers used in schematic diagrams, which is necessary for interpreting the secondary side equipment schematics referenced in the analysis question, while Section B discusses regulatory requirements for secondary coolant system iodine activity that must be considered when analyzing or designing such equipment.

Section A provides schematic and detailed drawings, including instrumentation and control diagrams, that visually clarify the design of secondary side equipment, while Section B specifies the regulatory requirements for instrumentation used in secondary coolant system leakage detection, thus Section A's drawings can be used to demonstrate compliance with Section B's requirements.

Section A and Section B both provide schematic diagrams (drawings) that visually clarify the design information for secondary side equipment related to a single NPM, with Section A focusing on electrical and I&C drawings and Section B focusing on piping and instrumentation diagrams, and both are referenced together in the same context and table.

Section A provides schematic and control drawings that visually clarify the design and layout of secondary side equipment, which are referenced or utilized in Section B's thermal-hydraulic performance testing to ensure the test setup accurately represents the actual system configuration.

Section A provides design guidelines and regulatory conformance for secondary side equipment (such as steam generators and decay heat removal systems), while Section B specifies the requirements and descriptions for instrumentation used to detect leakage in the secondary coolant system, thus Section A's equipment design must satisfy the monitoring and detection requirements outlined in Section B.

Section B provides the schematic diagrams and detailed drawings that visually represent the secondary side equipment of a single NPM, which are referenced in Section A as part of the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to demonstrate conformance with regulatory criteria.

Section A outlines regulatory design guidelines and their applicability to NuScale's secondary side systems, while Section B describes thermal-hydraulic performance testing conducted to validate the design and safety margins of those same systems, thus Section B provides supporting evidence for the adequacy of the design approach discussed in Section A.

Section A describes the design guidelines and regulatory conformance for secondary side equipment (specifically the auxiliary feedwater system or its equivalent in NuScale), while Section B outlines the requirements for instrumentation and monitoring of secondary coolant system leakage, both addressing different but related aspects of secondary side equipment in a single NPM.

Section A discusses regulatory design guidelines and their applicability to NuScale's secondary side systems, while Section B provides the actual schematic diagrams and drawings that visually represent the secondary side equipment referenced in Section A.

Section A outlines the design guidelines and regulatory context for secondary side equipment (specifically the DHRS as analogous to AFW), while Section B provides supporting thermal-hydraulic performance test data (CHF testing) that validates the design's technical adequacy, thereby supporting the schematic explanation required by the analysis question.

Section A provides schematic diagrams and drawings of the secondary side equipment for a single NPM, while Section B outlines regulatory design guidelines (such as BTP 10-2) that these schematics must conform to or address, specifically regarding issues like water hammer in steam generators.

Section A provides schematic diagrams and drawings of the secondary side equipment, which can be used to visually clarify and support the regulatory conformance claims discussed in Section B regarding design guidelines for auxiliary feedwater systems and their applicability to the NuScale DHRS.

Section A and Section B both reference BTP 10-2, Rev 4 and discuss its application to NuScale's secondary side equipment, specifically noting the applicability (or lack thereof) of auxiliary feedwater system guidelines to the NuScale DHRS, thus connecting them through their regulatory interpretation of design guidelines for secondary side equipment.

Section A provides the diagrammatic symbols and legends necessary to interpret the schematic diagrams of secondary side equipment, which are referenced in Section B's discussion of design features and instrumentation used for secondary coolant system leakage detection.
Section A provides the legend and symbol definitions necessary to interpret the schematic diagrams described in Section B, which include the secondary side equipment for a single NPM.

Section A provides the diagrammatic symbols and legends necessary to interpret the schematic diagrams of secondary side equipment, while Section B discusses thermal-hydraulic performance testing data that would be represented and contextualized using such diagrams.

Section A provides the specific diagram legends and symbols for instrumentation and accessory devices that are referenced in Section B's discussion of design features and instrumentation used for secondary coolant system leakage detection.

Section A provides the legend and symbol definitions necessary to interpret the schematic diagrams described in Section B, which include piping and instrumentation diagrams for the secondary side equipment of a single NPM.

Section A provides the diagram legends necessary to interpret schematic diagrams of secondary side equipment, while Section B discusses performance testing data relevant to the design and validation of components (such as the steam generator) that would be depicted in those diagrams, connecting them through the need to understand equipment representation for analysis.

Section A provides the symbol legend necessary to interpret schematic diagrams, which is a prerequisite for understanding the design features and instrumentation described in Section B for secondary side equipment.
Section A provides the symbol legend necessary to interpret the instrumentation and controls shown in the schematic diagrams described in Section B.

Section A provides the symbol legend necessary to interpret schematic diagrams, which is a prerequisite for understanding the secondary side equipment schematics discussed in the analysis question, while Section B describes performance testing data that would be represented in such schematics.

Section A and Section B both provide legends for interpreting symbols used in the piping and instrumentation diagrams (P&IDs) relevant to the secondary side equipment of a single NPM, with Section A focusing on valve and vent symbols and Section B detailing instrument and accessory device symbols, together specifying how to read and understand the schematic diagrams.

Section A provides the legend for piping and valve symbols used in P&IDs, while Section B provides the legend for instrumentation and control logic symbols, and together they specify how to interpret the schematic diagram of the secondary side equipment for a single NPM by defining the meaning of all graphical symbols used.

Section A provides the legends for piping and instrumentation diagram (P&ID) symbols, while Section B provides the legends for instrumentation and control (I&C) logic symbols, both of which are necessary to interpret the schematic diagram of the secondary side equipment for a single NPM.
